title: P0: audit and harden shell command execution
description: Config-sourced command strings are passed directly to sh -c in daemon.rs (lines 850-861 for main commands, 795 for health probe exec, 626-629 for shutdown commands). While this requires config file write access, it's risky on multi-user systems. Audit all command execution paths and consider validation or sandboxing options.

title: Create a skill for using decompose
description: Create a Claude Code skill that helps users interact with decompose - starting/stopping services, viewing logs, debugging issues. The skill should provide context about decompose commands and guide usage.
close_reason: Created .claude/skills/decompose.md with CLI reference, debugging workflows, compose file reference, and env var docs

title: Add agent guidance, skills, and project configuration
description: Set up CLAUDE.md improvements, hooks, custom skills, and any other agent tooling needed to make working on this project more efficient. Includes things like build/test commands, coding conventions, common workflows, and project-specific guidance.
close_reason: Added build/test/lint commands, testing patterns, new-command workflow, and commit message reference to CLAUDE.md

title: P1: enforce restrictive file and directory permissions
description: paths.rs:70-77 creates runtime/state dirs with default umask — could be world-readable. Daemon log file (daemon.rs:66-72) same issue. Should enforce 0o700 for directories and 0o600 for files to prevent information leakage.

title: P1: atomic socket/PID creation with advisory locking
description: daemon.rs:130-132 does check-then-remove-then-bind on the Unix socket with no atomic operation. Two concurrent 'decompose up' calls can collide. PID file (daemon.rs:168) has the same issue — no advisory lock. Should use flock() or equivalent to prevent duplicate daemons.

title: Readiness checks don't appear to work
description:
User-reported: readiness probes (readiness_probe in YAML) don't seem to actually drive the `healthy` flag. Needs repro + root-cause investigation.

## What to check

- Are probes actually being scheduled? Look at the supervisor/probe loop wiring in src/daemon.rs. There's probe-related code but no confirmation it fires on the configured period.
- `ProcessRuntime.healthy` — what sets it true, what sets it false, and is the probe actually updating it? grep for assignments to .healthy.
- Does `depends_on: condition: process_healthy` correctly gate dependent services?
- `ready_log_line` (log_ready flag) — separate path; also worth sanity-checking if behaving.
- HTTP probe: src/daemon.rs — is there even an HTTP client used to hit the endpoint? (No `reqwest`/`hyper`/`ureq` in Cargo.toml — might be the root cause for http_get probes specifically.)
- Exec probe: is the check command spawned at the right interval with the right timeout?
- Tests — tests/cli_integration.rs does have a health_check test that supposedly passes; figure out why the test passes but real-world usage doesn't. The test's probe might be trivial enough to not exercise the broken path.

## Repro steps needed

No concrete repro from the user yet. Confirm: which probe type (exec / http_get), what the service actually does, and what `decompose ps` reports as its `healthy` state. Could also be ready_log_line or a dependency gating issue being reported as 'readiness broken'.

## Possible outcomes

- If http_get probes: likely missing HTTP client entirely — easy root cause.
- If exec probes: timeout / signal / cwd issue.
- If log_ready: regex compilation, buffering, or partial-line handling.

title: Document compose YAML file format in README
description: Add comprehensive documentation of the compose YAML schema to the README, covering all supported fields, types, defaults, and examples. Reference the schema from CLAUDE.md but expand with user-facing examples.
close_reason: Added comprehensive YAML reference to README covering global settings, process config, dependencies, health probes, shutdown, env vars, and interpolation — all verified against source

title: Improve README with installation instructions
description: Add clear installation instructions (cargo install, building from source, platform support) to the README.
close_reason: Expanded README installation section with Rust version note, platform table, curl one-liner, flake input example, and from-source instructions

title: Add ls command
description: List running decompose instances by scanning the socket directory and pinging each daemon. (TASKS.md 1e)
close_reason: All implemented and merged to main

title: Add kill command
description: kill [-s SIGNAL] [SERVICE...] — send a signal immediately without running the shutdown sequence. Default SIGKILL. (TASKS.md 1d)
close_reason: All implemented and merged to main

title: Add config command
description: Validate and print the resolved config (YAML by default, JSON with --json). Useful for debugging merges and interpolation. (TASKS.md 1c)
close_reason: All implemented and merged to main

title: Promote -f/--file, -e/--env-file, --session, --disable-dotenv to true global flags
description: Move these flags from per-subcommand to Cli level. Unblocks logs -f (currently -f is taken by --file within LogsArgs). Matches docker compose -f FILE <subcmd> shape. (TASKS.md 1b)
close_reason: Promoted --file, --session, -e/--env-file, --disable-dotenv to global flags on Cli struct with global=true. Renamed GlobalArgs to OutputOnlyArgs. All tests pass.

title: ps: unify status and health into single state column with glyphs
description:
Combine the status and health columns in 'decompose ps' output into a single STATE column with colored glyphs:

- not_started: dash (-) in light gray
- pending: ⧖ pending in yellow (waiting on dependencies)
- running (not yet healthy): ◌ running in yellow
- healthy: ● healthy in green
- stopped: - stopped in light gray
- failed: ✕ failed in red

This simplifies the ps output and makes process state scannable at a glance.

title: test: expand coverage for race conditions, config merges, and edge cases
description:
Key testing gaps identified:
- No test for immediate-exit processes (before supervisor loop runs)
- No concurrent 'decompose up' race condition test
- No multi-file config merge edge cases
- No test for probe timeout > period
- Interpolation edge cases: double-escaped dollar-then-var, nested defaults
- No test for process group handling (child processes)
- No negative tests for malformed IPC JSON, invalid timeout values

title: fix: improve error handling consistency
description:
Error handling is inconsistent across the codebase:
- Config loading uses .with_context() but process spawn uses bare .to_string() (daemon.rs:378-390)
- Socket accept errors logged but never escalate (daemon.rs:209)
- Log file read failure silently returns empty (lib.rs:341-343)
- Shutdown command result silently ignored (daemon.rs:629) — needs a timeout too
- .env parsing silently skips malformed lines (config.rs:454) — should warn
- parse_signal() only supports 10 hardcoded signals (lib.rs:452-474)
- Regex fallback has unnecessary inner .unwrap() (daemon.rs:369,534)

title: config: make hardcoded timeouts configurable
description:
Several timeouts are hardcoded with no user override:
- 500ms poll interval and 5min daemon ready timeout (lib.rs:757-758)
- 5s IPC timeout (ipc.rs:67)
- 150ms supervisor tick (daemon.rs:315)
These should be configurable, especially for slow-starting services. Also add config validation: max replicas, probe timeout vs period sanity checks, dependency depth limits.

title: refactor: extract duplicated patterns across daemon and lib
description:
Several patterns are repeated multiple times:
- Shutdown 'send true to all controllers' broadcast repeated 3+ times in supervisor_loop and IPC handlers
- Readiness/liveness probe setup is nearly identical (daemon.rs:401-419)
- Service resolution logic repeated across Stop/Start/Kill/Restart handlers
- IPC response matching pattern (Ok(Response::Ack) => {}, Ok(Response::Error) => bail!) repeated ~5 times in lib.rs
Extract shared helpers to reduce duplication.

title: refactor: break up start_process() and run_up() into smaller functions
description: start_process() in daemon.rs:355-562 is 207 lines managing spawn, output readers, probes, lifecycle, and restart all in one function. run_up() in lib.rs:74-232 is 158 lines. Both should be decomposed into smaller, testable units. Also remove dead code at daemon.rs:444-445 (identical branches in select! arm).

title: logs: pipe output through $PAGER by default
description:
The `decompose logs` command should pipe output through $PAGER (or a sensible default like `less`) when stdout is a TTY, similar to how ripgrep and git handle paging. ripgrep uses `--no-pager` to opt out and respects $PAGER/$RIPGREP_PAGER env vars. Git similarly pages by default and offers `--no-pager`. For decompose logs, the behavior should be:

- When stdout is a TTY and not in follow mode (`-f`), pipe through $PAGER (defaulting to `less`)
- `--no-pager` flag to disable paging
- Skip paging when output is piped or redirected (non-TTY)
- Skip paging in follow mode (`-f`) since the stream is continuous
- Respect $PAGER environment variable for user customization

title: Simplify ps columns: short status, separate PID, drop RESTARTS
description:
User feedback on current ps TTY output: STATUS column's `running(pid=48777)`/`exited(code=1)` parenthetical clutter is unwanted, and the RESTARTS column isn't pulling its weight.

## New layout

    (glyph)  NAME     STATUS    PID     HEALTH
    ✓        alive    running   48777   —
    ✗        died     failed    -       —
    ✓        healthy  running   48781   ✓
    —        stopped  stopped   -       —

- STATUS column uses the short state word only — pull from `ProcessSnapshot.state` instead of `ProcessSnapshot.status`. Values: running / pending / restarting / exited / failed / failed_to_start / stopped / disabled / not_started.
- PID column: numeric pid for running processes; `-` otherwise.
- HEALTH column stays as-is (✓/✗/—).
- RESTARTS column removed.

## Implementation

- Add `pid: Option<u32>` field to ProcessSnapshot (additive JSON change — existing consumers unaffected).
- Daemon populates `pid` from `ProcessStatus::Running { pid }`; None otherwise.
- emit_ps swaps columns per above. Keep glyph column + replicas BASE column.
- Keep `status` string field in JSON for now (don't break existing JSON consumers that read it). The TTY just stops displaying it.
- Exit code is no longer visible in the TTY view; available via `--json` for consumers who need it.

title: Add state glyph to ps TTY output + fix stopped showing green
description:
Two related fixes to ps TTY output:

1. **Add a leading glyph column** so each ps row has a visual state indicator:
   - ✓ for running/exited-0
   - … for pending/starting/restarting
   - ✗ for failed / failed_to_start
   - — for stopped / disabled / not_started

2. **Make HEALTH column visually meaningful**: show ✓/✗/— based on (has_readiness_probe, healthy):
   - has probe + healthy → ✓ (green)
   - has probe + not healthy → ✗ (red)
   - no probe → — (dim)
   User reported health status 'not showing' — today it's just literal 'healthy' or '-' which is easy to miss.

3. **Fix 'stopped' rendering green**: `ProcessStatus::Stopped.to_json_status()` returns 'exited', which the styler maps to GREEN. Change it to return 'stopped' so the already-dim mapping in style_for_status kicks in.

JSON output change: the `state` field will now be 'stopped' for stopped processes instead of 'exited'. Update the integration test at tests/cli_integration.rs:546 that asserts `state == exited || failed` post-shutdown to include 'stopped'.

title: Polish `up` and `ps` TTY output with colors + a footer block
description:
Lightweight pass on TTY output; full TUI comes later. Scope is deliberately narrow.

## Add

- Colored status words using a minimal crate (anstyle preferred — already in the clap tree, zero extra deps of consequence). Respect NO_COLOR env var and skip ANSI when stdout isn't a terminal.
  - green: running / ready / healthy / exited-0
  - yellow: pending / starting / restarting
  - red: failed / exited-nonzero
  - dim: disabled / not_started
- A footer block after `up` completes (attached or detached), styled roughly:

      5 services · 7 processes · session staging               ctrl-c detaches
      daemon supervising · socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/decompose/

  (The right-side 'ctrl-c detaches' hint only shows in attached mode.)
- Replace the bland 'decompose started (pid 12345)' with a simple checkmark-prefixed one-liner; use a non-arrow glyph for 'starting' (e.g. `…` or `*` — not `▶`).

## Out of scope (deferred to the eventual TUI milestone)

- Progressive per-service status rows with timestamps
- Probe-type column (readiness/tcp:PORT/log:pattern/replicas:N/waiting:dep) — redundant with status
- Combining elapsed-ms and pid in one right-hand column — awkward; defer
- Live-updating board rendering

## Constraints

- JSON output MUST remain byte-identical — tests depend on it.
- Existing ps table columns stay; just color the STATUS / HEALTH cells.

title: `start` should work on services defined in config but not yet launched
description:
`decompose start serviceB` currently rejects serviceB with 'unknown service(s)' (src/daemon.rs:911-916, via resolve_services at src/daemon.rs:776) if serviceB wasn't part of the initial `up` subset. The service is defined in the config — it just was never selected — so the error is misleading.

Expected behavior: if a service exists in the merged config but isn't yet in daemon state, `start` should add it and launch it (same as the incremental `up` path). If the service truly isn't defined anywhere in the config, keep the 'unknown service' error.

Depends on decompose-mf9. Once the daemon holds the full config, `start` just needs to flip any non-launched-yet services into Pending (plus whatever bookkeeping the supervisor needs to actually spawn them).

title: `up` against a running daemon should start requested services incrementally
description:
Currently, after a daemon is already running, `decompose up -d serviceB` detects the daemon via Ping (src/lib.rs:88-92) and short-circuits with 'already_running' — serviceB is never started even if it's defined in the config. Docker compose, by contrast, treats `up` as idempotent and incremental: services already running are left alone, missing services get brought up.

Expected behavior: when the daemon is already running, forward the requested service list (and --no-deps) to it as a start-or-create request. Any services not yet in daemon state should be added (pulling their spec from the merged config); already-running services should be left alone; stopped/exited ones should be relaunched.

Depends on decompose-mf9 (daemon keeping the full config). If we go with the 'load everything, just don't launch' approach from that issue, this issue reduces to 'send a Start request for the named services' — though we may also need to refresh config from disk in case the YAML changed since the daemon started.

title: Daemon should load full config, not filter to initial subset
description:
When `decompose up -d serviceA` spawns the daemon, src/daemon.rs:140-142 calls filter_process_subset which drops every other service from DaemonState.processes. This makes later services invisible — `start serviceB` fails with 'unknown service' and `up -d serviceB` can't add it to the running environment.

Docker compose always knows about every service in the compose file; it just doesn't auto-start the ones you didn't ask for. Decompose should do the same: keep all config-defined services in daemon state, but mark non-selected ones as not-yet-started (e.g. a new ProcessStatus variant, or leave them Pending and suppress launch until asked).

This is the enabling change for the two follow-on issues (incremental `up` and `start` on unlaunched services).

title: Fix scale-down replica cleanup
description: handle_client signals excess replicas to stop but never removes them from the process map. (TASKS.md 5d)
close_reason: Scale command was previously removed; replica cleanup fix is moot without it. If scale is re-added, this should be addressed then.

title: Preserve daemon log across restarts
description: Currently truncated on every 'up', losing post-mortem logs. Add rotation or append mode. (TASKS.md 4i)

title: Add exec and run commands
description: exec SERVICE CMD... attaches a one-off command to a running service's environment; run SERVICE CMD... executes a one-off command in a service context without attaching. Useful for debugging (e.g., decompose exec db psql). (TASKS.md 1j)

title: Add --remove-orphans to up
description: If the config has removed a process, stop its remaining replicas in the running daemon. (TASKS.md 1i)

title: Add --wait to up -d
description: Block until all services are healthy (when readiness probes are configured) or started. (TASKS.md 1h)

title: Add --timeout to down
description: Override per-process shutdown.timeout_seconds at the CLI level. (TASKS.md 1g)

title: Daemon should auto-exit when orphaned (parent dead, no clients)
description:
When the process that spawned the daemon (e.g. `decompose up`, a test runner) dies without calling `down`, the daemon runs forever as an orphan. This happens regularly in integration tests that panic or get killed, and could also happen in normal usage (terminal closed, SSH dropped, etc.).

## Proposed fix

The daemon should periodically check whether it's been orphaned and self-terminate if so. Suggested heuristic:

1. On startup, record the parent PID (`getppid()` or passed via arg).
2. In the supervisor loop (or a dedicated watchdog task), periodically check if the parent PID is still alive (`kill(ppid, 0)` on Unix).
3. If the parent is dead AND no IPC client has connected in the last N seconds (e.g. 30s), initiate a graceful shutdown (same as `down`).

The 'no recent client' grace period prevents false positives: if the user ran `up -d` and closed the terminal, other terminals may still be using `ps`/`logs`/`start` against the daemon. Only if it's truly abandoned should it self-exit.

## Edge cases

- `up -d` in a script that exits immediately — parent dies right away but the daemon is intentionally long-lived. The IPC grace period handles this as long as the user interacts within the window.
- Alternatively, make this opt-in via a `--orphan-timeout` flag or config, defaulting to disabled for `up -d` and enabled for test/CI contexts.
- On macOS, `getppid()` returns 1 (launchd) once the parent dies — easy to detect.

## Also consider

- Test harness: add a `Drop` guard to `setup_project()` in tests/cli_integration.rs that calls `down` on panic. Doesn't cover SIGKILL but handles most test failures. Complementary to the daemon-side fix.

title: Add restart separator lines to process log output
description:
When a process restarts (via restart_policy), logs from the previous run persist in the daemon log — which is correct for a dev tool. But there's no visual boundary between runs, making it hard to tell where one crash ended and the next attempt began.

Add a separator line to the log stream when a process restarts, e.g.:

    [api] listening on :3000
    [api] error: connection reset
    [api] --- restarted (exit code 1, attempt 2/5) ---
    [api] listening on :3000

The separator should include:
- The exit code or signal that ended the previous run
- The restart attempt number (and max if configured)
- Visually distinct from normal log output (e.g. dim/grey when colored)

Implementation: in the restart path of the supervisor loop (src/daemon.rs, after a process exits and before the replacement is spawned), write a formatted separator line to the daemon log. The line should be prefixed with the process name like normal log lines so `decompose logs serviceName` filtering picks it up.

title: Split ready/alive flags on ProcessSnapshot so both probe types can coexist
description:
Flagged by the rqs fix agent. Today `readiness_probe` and `liveness_probe` both write to the single `ProcessRuntime.healthy` boolean — so if a service configures both, they stomp on each other's state. The readiness-probe result is what `depends_on: process_healthy` gates on and what `ps` reports as HEALTH, but liveness also needs to track pass/fail per probe cycle to decide when to restart.

Fix: give ProcessRuntime separate `ready` and `alive` flags. Expose both via ProcessSnapshot (additive JSON fields — don't break existing consumers). `depends_on: process_healthy` continues to gate on readiness. HEALTH column stays readiness-driven; consider adding a LIVENESS column to ps table later.

Punted during decompose-rqs because it required an IPC/JSON schema change beyond the scope of the bug fix. The current state works correctly as long as services don't use both probe types simultaneously.

title: Add shell autocompletion for the decompose CLI
description:
Ship completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, powershell, elvish — same shells clap_complete supports out of the box.

## Approach

decompose already uses clap, so the standard path is `clap_complete`:

1. **Generation command** — expose a hidden/opt-in subcommand (e.g. `decompose completion <shell>`) that prints a completion script to stdout. Lets users run `decompose completion zsh > ~/.zfunc/_decompose` or pipe into fpath. This is the runtime fallback.

2. **Pre-generated scripts in build.rs** — write completion files to `$OUT_DIR/completions/` during build using `clap_complete::generate_to`. This is what ripgrep does (see ripgrep's `build.rs`).

3. **Distribution**:
   - Include the pre-generated scripts in the release tarball under `complete/` so homebrew/apt/port packagers can install them to the right shell dirs.
   - homebrew formula can then drop them into `$(brew --prefix)/share/{bash-completion/completions,zsh/site-functions,fish/vendor_completions.d}`.
   - Document the manual install path in the README for users on other systems.

4. **Dynamic completions** where clap's static completions aren't enough — service names (for `start`/`stop`/`logs SERVICE...`), session names (for `--session NAME`), and config file paths. For service names, the completion script can shell out to `decompose config --json | jq -r '.processes | keys[]'` or similar; for sessions, `decompose ls --json`. Prefer native clap_complete dynamic support (clap_complete's `engine` feature, currently unstable) if its churn is acceptable, otherwise hand-write the dynamic bits into each shell script post-generation.

## References

- clap_complete: https://docs.rs/clap_complete
- ripgrep build.rs: https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/blob/master/build.rs
- ripgrep release workflow packs completions into the tarball

## Acceptance

- `decompose completion <shell>` prints a working script for at least bash/zsh/fish.
- `cargo build` produces pre-generated scripts under target/.../build/.../out/completions/ (or wherever).
- README has install instructions.
- Service-name completion works for `start` / `stop` / `restart` / `kill` / `logs` at least in zsh and bash.
